Details:
Several bench-scale studies found moderate removal of natural organic matter through biological treatment. The maximum removals reported in studies with contact times of up to 3 hours were 46 percent of DOC [2450, 2449] and 49 percent of BDOC [2445]. One bench-scale study reported 97 to 99 percent removal of TOC with a contact time of 24 hours [2448].
A pilot-scale study using a membrane biological reactor incorporating powdered activated carbon (PAC) found up to 33 percent removal of DOC and 51 percent removal of BDOC. Without PAC, the reactor achieved only 6 to 16 percent removal of DOC and 29 percent removal BDOC [2453].
